response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering skills with 15+ years of experience and proficiency in C and Linux, they fatally lack the critical native-level Japanese language proficiency explicitly required for the role. Additionally, the resume shows no evidence of embedded networking, security technologies (encryption, authentication protocols, attack detection), kernel-level work, or firmware development. Recent career trajectory (past 5 years) has focused on web and cloud development, moving away from embedded systems entirely. The domain expertise required for security-focused embedded networking firmware is fundamentally absent.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience with exposure to C/C++, Linux, and embedded systems at National Instruments and Altom Consulting
Strong software engineering practices emphasizing testing, code quality, and CI/CD, with demonstrated ability to learn diverse technology stacks
Evidence of low-level systems work including custom kernel understanding and infrastructure management (GitLab, CI servers, Docker)
concerns: No native-level Japanese language proficiency listed; resume explicitly states English (Fluent), Romanian (Fluent), German (Basic) only—fails hard requirement
Complete absence of embedded networking, network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP, NETCONF), or switch firmware domain experience
Recent 5-year career pivot away from embedded systems toward web/cloud stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS, MongoDB); C experience last active in 2014–2015

key_factors.factor: Embedded networking and security domain expertise
C and Linux experience (dated but present)
